cap_space_notes: Post-FA cap space per OverTheCap as of March 16. Eagles used the Jordan Davis extension and Landon Dickerson restructure to create additional cap room; Woolen's cap-manipulated deal ($3.4M 2026 hit via void years) and Goedert re-sign further helped. Pre-FA figure is an approximation from pre-legal-tampering reports.

key_departures.impact: Top pass rusher on the roster after the midseason trade. Signed a 4-year, $120M deal with the Panthers ($30M/yr AAV, $80M guaranteed). Eagles were unwilling to match at that price. Leaves a significant hole at EDGE. Nolan Smith and Jalyx Hunt remain but both are undersized. Phillips played only 8 regular-season games with PHI after the trade deadline deal.

Starting safety and 2025 team captain departs. Was an UDFA home run for the Eagles. No clear starting-caliber replacement currently on roster. Sydney Brown and Marcus Epps are candidates but neither is proven. Creates a legitimate starter-level void at S.

Young starting LB departs on a 3-year, $36M Raiders deal. Eagles could not match due to cap constraints. However, 2025 draft pick Jihaad Campbell is viewed as his internal replacement. Departure is notable but not back-breaking given Campbell's presence.

Former first-round pick acquired in 2024 trade. Had 37 catches for 478 yards over two seasons with PHI. Depth/rotational WR loss. AJ Brown and DeVonta Smith remain as the top two. Dotson did not solve a need — his departure is a mild depth hit at WR.
Injury-limited rotational EDGE (played only 3 games in 2025 with PHI). Departure adds to the thin EDGE room but was not a reliable contributor.
Had 1 sack and 15 QB pressures in 12 games as a rotational EDGE. Departure further thins the EDGE group.
Backup TE with 7 catches for 40 yards in 2025. Depth loss, mostly replaced by Mundt signing.
Reserve guard/tackle who started 1 game in 2025. Pure depth loss. Eagles' five starting OL all return. Minimal impact on needs picture.
Versatile backup OL who made 4 starts in 2025. Pure depth departure. Roster body, not a need-driver.
Emergency/backup QB. Tanner McKee remains under contract as QB2 behind Jalen Hurts. Pure depth loss with no need implications.
Started at CB2 opposite Quinyon Mitchell for most of 2025 after Ringo's struggles. Now a UFA. Departure directly created the CB2 vacancy that Woolen was signed to fill.

signings.analysis: Blockbuster extension for the 26-year-old nose tackle. 3-year, $78M with $65M guaranteed (most guaranteed money in NFL history for a nose tackle). Davis is locked through 2029. Coming off a career year (4.5 sacks, 72 tackles, 9 TFLs in 2025). IDL need is completely addressed for the foreseeable future alongside Jalen Carter, who is up for his own extension. This is a full lock-down of a position of strength.

One-year, $12M fully guaranteed deal (max value up to $15M with incentives). Woolen is the projected CB2 starter opposite Quinyon Mitchell with Cooper DeJean in the slot. Former Pro Bowler (6 INTs as a rookie in 2022) and a Super Bowl LX champion with Seattle. The deal is fully guaranteed and at starter-AAV for CB, but the one-year structure signals a bridge/prove-it season. The Eagles still have long-term CB2 uncertainty — they will need to invest in this position in the draft for 2027 and beyond. Partially addresses CB need; doesn't fully resolve it long-term.

Re-signed on a 1-year, $7M deal ($4.25M signing bonus). Goedert had a career-best 2025 (60 rec, 591 yds, 11 TDs — franchise TE TD record). He's back as TE1 for 2026, but the one-year structure at below-market TE money (starter-tier AAV is $8-14M; $7M is below that for a starter-level player) and his age (31) mean this is a bridge year, not a solution. The Eagles still need to find a long-term TE heir, likely via the draft. Partially resolves the near-term TE need; the long-term need remains real.

One-year deal, max value $7.3M with $4.3M guaranteed. Replaces some of the EDGE depth lost (Phillips, Uche, Ojulari) but is a rotational player, not a starter. Had back-to-back 6-sack seasons in 2023-24 before falling to 2 sacks in 2025. Joins Nolan Smith and Jalyx Hunt as the three primary EDGEs. EDGE remains a significant need — the room lacks a true big-bodied pass rusher and is thin. A one-year depth signing does not resolve what Phillips' departure created. Need remains elevated.

signings.contract_notes: Exact financials not publicly reported; estimated at vet-minimum/low-cost level per market context and reports of a standard one-year deal.
signings.analysis: One-year deal. Elite blocking TE (7th-highest PFF pass-blocking grade among TEs with 100+ blocking snaps in 2025). Directly addresses the Eagles' lack of a blocking TE — a noted weakness in their 2025 run game. Has familiarity with new OC Sean Mannion from their Rams and Vikings overlap. Pure depth/role player signing. Provides important blocking complement to Goedert and Calcaterra but is not a TE1-caliber player.

signings.contract_notes: Exact financials not publicly reported; estimated at low-end vet level per reports of a standard one-year deal.
signings.analysis: Re-signed for a fifth season on a cheap one-year deal. Pass-catching TE backup to Goedert. Has 42 career catches for 494 yards. Primarily provides depth continuity and special teams. Does not solve any structural need — merely keeps a known quantity in house for another year.

signings.contract_notes: Exact financials not publicly reported; estimated at vet-minimum/low-cost level based on player age, role, and market context.
signings.analysis: One-year deal. 10-year NFL veteran (9 years with Patriots, 1 with Commanders). Slot/outside depth CB3 behind Mitchell, Woolen, and DeJean. Connection to new Eagles secondary coach Mike Pellegrino (coached Jones in NE 2019-24). Pure depth signing that does not change the CB need picture materially. Provides experienced insurance behind a (still somewhat thin) cornerback group.

4-year, $14M extension ($7M guaranteed), keeping the punter through 2029. Mann had a franchise-record 49.9 yards/punt average in 2025. Fifth-highest paid punter in the NFL by AAV. Special teams need is fully locked. No draft-need implications.

contract_restructures.note: Restructured to a revised 2-year deal for approximately $36M. Previously set to earn $39M over two seasons. Deal shortened by one year (no longer under contract for 2028), providing the Eagles with cap relief in 2026. Dickerson remains the starting LG. IOL need not created.

Contract renegotiated to avoid a cut. Was slated for $10.1M cap hit; restructured to effectively a 1-year deal worth ~$2.765M with a $1.42M 2026 cap hit. Remains as a depth DB/nickel/safety hybrid option for 2026.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Jordan Davis 3yr/$78M extension ($65M guaranteed) locks him through 2029 as the highest-paid nose tackle in NFL history. Jalen Carter (2nd IDL) also under contract with 5th-year option decision pending. IDL is a position of elite depth, not a need. Draft at IDL only for future developmental depth, not as a priority.

Jaelan Phillips (starter) signed with Carolina ($30M/yr). Azeez Ojulari and Joshua Uche (both rotational) also departed. Only Nolan Smith (eligible for 5th-year option) and Jalyx Hunt (under contract through 2027) remain as established EDGEs. Arnold Ebiketie (1yr/$7.3M) is a rotational addition, not a Phillips replacement. The group lacks a true big-bodied edge presence. EDGE escalates to a top-tier draft need — likely picks 1 or 2 in PHI's priority board.

Reed Blankenship (starter, captain) departed to Houston on a 3-year/$24.75M deal. No replacement was signed in free agency. Sydney Brown and Marcus Epps are the internal candidates — neither is a proven starter. Michael Carter II (restructured) can play in the secondary but is primarily a nickel/hybrid. Safety is now a genuine starter-level need that may require draft investment in rounds 2-3.

Riq Woolen (1yr/$12M fully guaranteed) fills the CB2 vacancy left by Adoree' Jackson's departure. Mitchell-Woolen-DeJean is a quality starting trio for 2026. Jonathan Jones provides experienced depth. However, Woolen's one-year deal means CB2 is NOT a solved long-term need. The Eagles should still target a developmental CB in the mid-rounds for 2027 insurance. Need drops from acute (tier 2) to moderate (tier 3), not eliminated.

Dallas Goedert (re-sign, 1yr/$7M) remains the TE1 for 2026. Mundt (blocking depth) and Calcaterra (pass-catching depth) round out the room. The 2026 season is covered. However, Goedert is 31 on a bridge deal and this is likely his final Eagles season. The Eagles have repeatedly deferred on finding a long-term TE heir — that need drops to a priority in the draft (rounds 2-4). TE is no longer a free agency emergency but remains a critical draft target for the future.

Nakobe Dean departed to Las Vegas (3yr/$36M). However, Jihaad Campbell was drafted specifically as Dean's eventual successor and Zack Baun (starter) remains under contract. The Eagles' LB room is not as depleted as it could be. Moderate need for depth/development rather than an immediate starter. Drop one tier.

Jahan Dotson (rotational) departed to Atlanta, but AJ Brown and DeVonta Smith remain among the NFL's best WR duos. Dotson was a depth piece. No WR was added in free agency. Need is unchanged — still a modest depth concern, and there is swirling speculation around an AJ Brown trade that could escalate this need significantly if that materializes.

All five starting OL return (Lane Johnson, Jordan Mailata, Cam Jurgens, Landon Dickerson, Tyler Steen). Dickerson was restructured, not replaced. Pryor and Toth (depth) departed but were not starters. The starting group is intact but aging/injury-prone (Dickerson's health is a concern). IOL depth is a legitimate mid-round draft target but the starting need is unchanged from pre-FA.

key_additions.headline: Record-setting extension locks up nose tackle through 2029 as highest-paid NT in NFL history
Former Pro Bowl CB and Super Bowl LX champion fills CB2 vacancy opposite All-Pro Quinyon Mitchell
Franchise-record 11-TD tight end returns for 9th Eagles season on bridge deal
Pass-rush rotation reinforcement adds depth to a thin EDGE group post-Phillips departure
Elite blocking TE (top-10 PFF blocking grades) fills key run-game void, familiar with new OC Mannion
Franchise-record punter locked through 2029 on longest punter deal in Eagles history

key_losses.headline: Top pass rusher walks on massive $30M/yr deal Eagles declined to match, leaving a gaping EDGE need
Starting safety and team captain departs without a replacement signed, creating a new starter-level void
Young starting LB leaves; Jihaad Campbell projects as internal replacement
CB2 starter from 2025 departs as UFA; Woolen signing directly replaces him
Rotational WR depth piece departs; Brown and Smith remain as the elite WR1/2 duo

additional_context.draft_capital: Eagles hold 9 picks in the 2026 NFL Draft including picks at Round 1 (No. 23), Round 2 (54), two Round 3 picks (68 from Haason Reddick trade, 98 compensatory), two Round 4 picks (122 own, 137 compensatory), and multiple Day 3 selections. Four compensatory picks reflect 2025 free agency losses.
pending_decisions: AJ Brown trade speculation remains active — a Brown trade would escalate WR from tier 3 to tier 1 instantly
Brandon Graham (38, EDGE) has expressed interest in playing a 17th season — if signed, adds minimal rotational depth at EDGE
Jalen Carter 5th-year option ($27.13M) decision due by May 1 — expected to be picked up; Carter extension negotiations will follow
Nolan Smith Jr. 5th-year option decision also pending before May 1
additional_context.philosophy_note: Roseman's stated 2026 offseason approach: prioritize homegrown extensions (Davis, Carter) and maximize 2027 compensatory picks by limiting outside free agent signings. This explains the restrained external spending despite multiple starter-level departures.
